title: SpaceX to raise at least $75 billion in IPO
summary: SpaceX plans to raise at least $75 billion in its IPO, valuing the company at over $1.75 trillion. This marks one of the largest public offerings in corporate history.

title: NASA declares end to MAVEN Mars mission
summary: NASA has officially ended the MAVEN Mars mission after losing contact with the spacecraft for six months. The investigation into the spacecraft's demise is still ongoing.

title: AST SpaceMobile sees New Glenn setback delaying initial commercial service into 2027
summary: AST SpaceMobile's initial commercial service for its direct-to-smartphone constellation is delayed into the first half of 2027 due to Blue Origin's New Glenn launchpad explosion. The setback is expected to push the timeline by three to six months.

title: After cooperation on SMILE mission, ESA and China chart parallel but separate paths
summary: ESA and China successfully launched the joint SMILE magnetosphere mission after a decade of cooperation, but future collaborations remain unlikely despite shared goals.

title: Electronics manufacturer Murata to explore Xona satellite timing service for telecom, data centers
summary: Murata is evaluating Xona's satellite timing service to enhance backup capabilities for GPS-dependent systems in telecom and data centers. Xona is developing a low Earth orbit network to provide alternative timing solutions.

title: Europe is rearming together — except in space
summary: Europe is strengthening its security measures in response to Russian aggression and concerns about U.S. commitment, but space defense remains an exception. The continent is prioritizing collective defense but has yet to fully integrate space capabilities into this strategy.

title: Muon Space unveils Starship-class satellite platform for orbital data centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a Starship-class satellite platform tailored for orbital data centers, with its first launch planned for 2028 after securing customers.

title: Blue Origin seeks to resume New Glenn launches by year’s end
summary: Blue Origin aims to resume New Glenn rocket launches by the end of the year after assessing launch pad damage as less severe than initially feared. The company's CEO indicated the rocket could return to flight operations within months.

title: NRO nominee says commercial space, AI are reshaping spy satellite agency
summary: Roger Mason, during a confirmation hearing, stated that commercial space and AI are transforming the space intelligence market for the NRO. He emphasized the need for clear government demand signals amid private investment growth.

title: Global SSA Market to Reach $61B as Governments Prioritize Space Security, Resilience, and Orbital Safety
summary: The global Space Situational Awareness (SSA) market is projected to reach $61 billion, driven by government focus on space security, resilience, and orbital safety. Novaspace's latest SSA report highlights a pivotal decade ahead for the industry.

title: Can black holes turn into white holes? It's not such a crazy idea, scientists say
summary: New research suggests primordial black holes from the Big Bang could live long enough to transform into white holes, spewing energy. Scientists propose this transition as a plausible, though unproven, cosmic phenomenon.

title: Forget 'Masters of the Universe', the best live-action He-Man and Skeletor encounter was in an insurance commercial
summary: A nostalgic insurance commercial features a live-action encounter between He-Man and Skeletor as ballroom dancers. The ad humorously reimagines the iconic characters in a new setting.

title: 'The mirror passed with flying colors': NASA just took its last look at the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope before launch
summary: NASA engineers completed the final inspection of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope's primary mirror and are preparing to ship it to Florida for launch. The telescope has passed all checks and is on track for its upcoming mission.

title: Our choice for the best deep-space viewing telescope — now with $200 off, this telescope deal is worth grabbing fast
summary: A deep-space viewing telescope, the Celestron NexStar 8SE, is currently discounted by $200 during an Amazon Prime Day deal. The telescope received high praise in an expert review.

title: Blue Origin says New Glenn rocket will launch again 'before the end of the year' after explosion
summary: Blue Origin aims to relaunch its New Glenn rocket before the end of 2026 after a recent explosion, with CEO Dave Limp setting an ambitious timeline for repairs and another launch attempt.

title: Sun erupts with 3 colossal solar flares in less than 24 hours, boosting chances for northern lights
summary: The Sun emitted three significant solar flares within 24 hours, causing radio blackouts on Earth and potentially enhancing northern lights displays due to incoming solar storms.

title: Like 'the loss of a loved one': NASA's Mars orbiter MAVEN is officially dead after months of radio silence
summary: NASA's MAVEN Mars orbiter has been declared dead after months of lost contact due to a Red Planet anomaly. The spacecraft, which studied Mars' atmosphere, has reached the end of its mission.

title: Spain's total solar eclipse 2026 comes with a catch — here's how to avoid ruining your view
summary: Spain's total solar eclipse in 2026 will occur near sunset over a complex landscape, requiring eclipse chasers to plan carefully. The event is set for Aug. 12, 2026.

title: Happy Pride Month! Remembering Sally Ride's historic legacy | Space photo of the day for June 3, 2026
summary: Sally Ride made history as the first American woman in space in June 1983 and was later revealed to be an LGBTQ trailblazer. The news highlights her legacy during Pride Month.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4908-4912: Goodbye Campo Marte, It’s Been Fun!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover successfully completed its 47th drill operation, named 'Campo Marte,' on Mars. The mission team utilized the stationary period to conduct additional scientific activities.

title: NASA Finds New Way Earth May Have Received Elements Needed for Life
summary: NASA-supported scientists discovered a new mechanism for how early Earth may have received elements essential for life, suggesting Jupiter played a role in their distribution. The findings, published in Science Advances, provide insights into the solar system's formation and habitability.

title: International Sea Level Satellite Observes El Niño Precursor
summary: The Sentinel-6 Michael Freilich satellite observed a warm Kelvin wave, a precursor to El Niño, in sea level data from March to May 2026. The wave, characterized by higher and warmer water, moved toward the coasts of Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.

title: Journey to the Center of the Virgo Cluster
summary: NASA/ESA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the active spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88) as it journeys through the Virgo Cluster. M88, located 63 million light-years away, is an active galaxy with a dynamic center.

title: NASA Drains 66-Million-Gallon Reservoir to Upgrade Critical Water System
summary: NASA drained a 66-million-gallon reservoir at Stennis Space Center to upgrade a critical water system used during rocket engine tests. The High Pressure Industrial Water Facility's reservoir was lowered to its lowest level since the 1960s.

title: NASA Says Farewell to MAVEN Mars Mission, Hosts Media Call Today
summary: NASA's MAVEN mission, which studied the Martian atmosphere for over 11 years, has concluded after exceeding its primary mission duration by a decade. The spacecraft lost contact with Earth in December after an unexpected signal loss.

title: Typhoon Jangmi
summary: Typhoon Jangmi is expected to bring heavy rainfall across southern Japan. The storm's impact is anticipated to affect a large region.

title: NASA Space Roboticist Challenge
summary: NASA is launching a robotic arm with seven degrees of freedom to low Earth orbit as part of the Fly Foundational Robots (FFR) mission. U.S. researchers, including principal investigators and graduate students, can access the robotic arm for their experiments.

title: Look Up!
summary: ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot and NASA astronaut Jack Hathaway monitored the automated docking of a SpaceX Dragon cargo spacecraft to the International Space Station from the ISS cupola. The event occurred on May 17, 2026, while the station was orbiting 259 miles above Earth.

title: NASA Testing Wastewater Treatment Facility for Future Moon Base
summary: NASA has sent a mobile wastewater treatment system to the University of North Dakota for testing under lunar-like conditions. The technology aims to support long-duration missions on the Moon and Mars.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) is assessing damage and potential future risks related to an anomaly. The Eastern Range, operated by SLD 45, has been cleared for operations.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparations for the mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and lunar landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week. Five missions are planned, with SpaceX and Chinese organizations leading the launches.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. This discovery enhances understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a major anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36, resulting in an explosion. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's efforts to develop its heavy-lift launch vehicle.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, involving rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative rebrands existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo project among the highlights. The launches are part of a busy manifest filled with commercial space missions.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: The International Space Station's Expedition 74 crew has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research aboard the orbital outpost.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway, supporting scientific research in space. The programme facilitates participation of research institutes in European space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and ensuring materials can withstand the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section supports these efforts with innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance accessibility and innovation in space activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses a sturdy and sensitive component in the space industry, though specific details are not provided.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test or experiment, referred to as 'The great parachute bake-out'.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling improved docking and debris capture. This innovation could enhance satellite servicing and space debris mitigation efforts.

title: Muon Space Unveils New, Larger Satellite Bus
summary: Muon Space has introduced a new, larger satellite bus named Condor-Ultra, which is three times the size of its predecessor Condor-XL. This new bus is designed to cater to the emerging orbital data center market.

title: NASA Reverts to Original CLD Procurement Plan
summary: NASA has decided to return to its original Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) procurement strategy. The move reflects industry feedback shaping the agency's approach.

title: Blue Origin Commits to Return to Flight This Year
summary: Blue Origin has announced plans to resume flight operations by the end of the year after assessing damage to its launch infrastructure. CEO Dave Limp confirmed the return-to-flight timeline on social media.

title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
summary: The House's annual defense authorization bill proposes eliminating the Space Development Agency (SDA) and Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO) within the Pentagon, which are tasked with accelerating space acquisition. This move could significantly impact the U.S. military's space capabilities and procurement strategies.

title: Northrop Grumman Taps Apex for Golden Dome SBI Collab
summary: Northrop Grumman has partnered with Apex to collaborate on the Golden Dome Space-Based Interceptor (SBI) project, aiming to deliver on-orbit missile defense capabilities by 2027.

title: Voyager Will Acquire Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Space Holdings will acquire Astrobotic to expand its lunar infrastructure capabilities. The deal aims to support NASA's Artemis program by providing end-to-end lunar surface solutions.

title: Impulse Space Closes $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space has secured $500 million in a Series D funding round, aiming to continue its current trajectory without expanding into new business lines or ventures. The company's President and COO emphasized the focus on scaling existing operations rather than diversifying.

title: Bellatrix Aerospace Tapped to Build Korean VLEO Demo Sat
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace is partnering with Korean optical payload manufacturer TelePIX to build a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) demonstration satellite, with a planned launch no earlier than 2028.

title: Opinion: Commercial Space Is Generating Signals Decision-Makers Cannot Hear
summary: Commercial space activities are producing vast operational data, but decision-makers are struggling to process these signals effectively. The current system fails to ensure critical information reaches those who need it.

title: Nuggets in the SpaceX S-1 and Our Thoughts on the Business
summary: SpaceX's IPO prospectus has been released, providing insights into the company's business. The document highlights key financial and operational details of SpaceX.

title: Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a new high-power satellite platform named Condor-Ultra, aimed at supporting orbital data centers. The first mission for this platform is scheduled for 2025.

title: Beyond Gravity Releases New Propulsion Pointing Mechanism
summary: Beyond Gravity has introduced a new ultra-light propulsion pointing mechanism designed for high maneuverability in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) constellations. The two-axis mechanism aims to enhance the performance and efficiency of satellite propulsion systems.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense IPOs to Raise $650M
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense successfully completed an IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, raising $650 million by offering 32.5 million shares. The company, which operates in the aerospace and defense sector, began trading publicly on Wednesday.

title: Assessing SpaceX Finances, Addressable Market, and the AI Pitch Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX is preparing for an IPO next week, with analysts evaluating its financials, market potential, and AI-related business ahead of the public offering. The company's filings have provided insights into its growth and strategic direction.

title: Impulse Space Raises Another Massive Funding Round in $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space raised $500 million in a Series D funding round led by 137 Ventures and Banner VC. This significant investment will support the company's continued development in space transportation and in-space services.

title: Iceye U.S. Appoints ASRC Federal Exec Ann Stevens as CEO
summary: Iceye U.S. has appointed Ann Stevens as CEO of its subsidiary, replacing Eric Jensen who now serves as COO. Stevens comes from ASRC Federal to lead the company's U.S. operations.

title: MDA Space to Supply Antennas to BAE Systems for Epoch 2 Missile Warning Constellation
summary: MDA Space has been selected by BAE Systems to supply antennas for the Epoch 2 missile warning constellation for the U.S. Space Force. The subcontract involves MDA Space designing and delivering the necessary components.

title: Juniper Research Expects Massive Spike in Direct-to-Cell Subscribers
summary: Juniper Research predicts a significant increase in direct-to-cell (D2C) subscribers, with over 130 million active monthly users expected by 2031. The forecast is detailed in the report 'Direct to Cell Market 2026 – 2031'.

title: Are Orbital Data Centers the Next Frontier of AI Infrastructure?
summary: The space industry is exploring orbital data centers as a potential solution for AI infrastructure, with competition among hyperscalers in cloud, AI, and space sectors. This trend could redefine future computing and data processing capabilities.

title: Sighting the Dark Fleet: Hyperspectral’s Role in Securing Safer Seas
summary: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation to U.S. and Israeli strikes, escalating a regional conflict. Hyperspectral technology is highlighted for its role in securing maritime operations amid the crisis.
